info.title: MiR Fleet Integration API v1
info.description:
This API provides RESTful endpoints, as well as events through webhooks. Use the API to integrate MiR Fleet with other systems.

Please refer to the MiR Fleet Enterprise Documentation on the MiR Support Portal for an introduction to the API.

This page serves as a reference for endpoints and data types.

get.description: Get a list of all groups and the content of each group.

post.description:
List the missions you would like to receive an export file of. You can use this file to transfer missions from one MiR Fleet application to another MiR Fleet without overwriting the whole site.

When you export missions, any missions nested in selected missions are also exported automatically. The export function does not support arguments in missions. Before exporting, assign values to all parameters. You can change the value back into arguments after exporting and importing the missions.

This feature is only supported in the API. You cannot import or export missions through the MiR Fleet interface.

200.description: Returns a .mission file with the selected missions. The .mission file can be imported to other MiR Fleet applications to share the same missions.

post.description:
Upload a .mission file to MiR Fleet to import missions you exported from another MiR Fleet application.

This feature is only supported in the API. You cannot import or export missions through the MiR Fleet interface.

get.description: Get log of terminated orders, as a CSV file. By default, orders terminated in the last hour are included.

parameters.name: oldestTerminationHour
parameters.description: Number of hours to look back - default one hour, must be between 1 and 24

get.description: Get a list of all robots connected to MiR Fleet and their system information.

post.description: Create a new serial-order.

patch.description: Update the priority, start-time, or assigned robot of a serial order.

put.summary: Update Phase of the Serial Order
put.description: Update the phase or phase arguments of a serial order.

delete.description: Abort a specific serial order by specifying its id in the endpoint.
Abort a specific serial order's fallback mission by specifying its id in the endpoint.

get.description: Get a list of all active orders in MiR Fleet. This includes scheduled order and orders currently being executed.

post.description: Create a new map using geoJSON data.

409.description: Conflict: A map with the specified values already exists in the database.

delete.description: Delete a map based on the map id.

post.description: Create a position of the following types: BasePosition, Charger, Marker, UtilityPosition.

patch.description: Update or modify a position or marker.

delete.description: Delete a position based on its position id.

get.summary: Export a site file from Fleet
get.description: Download the current site as a .site file.

post.summary: Import a site file to MiR Fleet.
post.description:
You must submit the file using these headers:

Content-Type: multipart/form-data

Content-Disposition: form-date; name=”site-file”

409.description: Conflict: A site is already being imported

200.description: Returns a snapshot of all site data and missions.

post.description: Lock or unlock a zone.
All subscriptions you have set up send a snapshot event of their event type — see Snapshots in the MiR Fleet Enterprise manual.

Create a subscription to an event. Specify the address of the endpoint you want MiR Fleet to publish the event to when it is triggered.

Each subscription must have a unique combination of base URL, event-type, and endpoint path.

See Callbacks for a definition of each event.

409.description: Conflict: The specified values already exists in the database.

post.description: Receive an event when an alert is triggered in the system.

Receive an event when an error occurs in MiR Fleet Integration API. This is only related to system errors that can occur when you send API requests. It does not catch any robot errors or errors in the request you sent in the API.
Recieve an event when a group in created, changed, or deleted.

Receive events when a Send JSON payload action is executed by a robot or the robot in a mission or Integration zone—see Integration actions and Zones in the MiR Fleet Enterprise manual. The robot can pass a customized JSON body in the event.
Receive events when a robot's software version or name changes.
Receive events with robot coordinates and when the robot enters Emergency or Protective stop. This subscription is a data stream. An event is posted to subscribed endpoints every second.
Receive events when a robot changes state. An event is only triggered when the robot changes states, not battery percentage.
Receive events whenever a phase or serial-order changes status.
Receive events when a map or map element is changed.
Receive an event when a subscription in the system changes status.
Receive an event when a robot changes connection status or an evacuation changes status.
Receive custom events defined using the Top Module Integration API.

204.description: No content: All subscriptions have been removed.

get.summary: Get list of all active subscriptions

200.description: Returns a list of subscriptions in the same format as the subscription request bodies — see POST api/v1/subscription.

post.description: Unsubscribe all subscriptions to a selected base-URL.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of all event-types in string format.
get.description: Note: This endpoint is deprecated. Please see available event types under the Subscribe endpoint callbacks instead.

200.description: Returns an array of strings of all the Event types you can subscribe to.

get.description:
Retrieve a list of all event-contracts in string format. The event-contracts describe the schemas of the event data that is sent to the subscribed endpoint.
            
Note: This endpoint is deprecated. Please see event definitions under the Subscribe endpoint callbacks instead.

post.description: Create an evacuation order.

post.summary: Generate an error log on MiR Fleet.

get.description: Get the current status of the MiR Fleet application.

200.description: Returns the status of the Authentication, MiR Fleet health, and the MiR Fleet database.

post.description: Make a robot that is paused in a Wait for Continue action to continue to the next action in the mission.

get.description: Get the software version of the MiR Fleet

post.summary: Send an event to the top module API

requestBody.description: An object describing the event to be sent and the target robot

tags.name: serial-order
tags.description: Start or update serial-orders on MiR Fleet.

tags.name: site
tags.description: Retrieve or update maps, positions, and missions.

tags.name: subscription
tags.description: Set up subscriptions to events. Set up a webhook client to monitor the events you subscribe and unsubscribe to.

tags.name: system
tags.description: Error logs, evacuations, and system information.
